data.title: Tower Semiconductor and Xanadu expand partnership to scale photonic quantum hardware
data.description:
• Tower Semiconductor (TSEM) on Thursday said it has expanded its collaboration with Canada based quantum computing firm Xanadu to develop advanced silicon photonics for fault-tolerant quantum computers.
  • The companies said the work builds on prior joint tapeouts and focuses on developing silicon photonics using Tower’s high-volume manufacturing platform.
  • Xanadu and Tower have co-engineered a production process and material stack designed to support scalable, large-scale photonic quantum systems.
  • Current work centers on optimizing key components, including ultra-low loss silicon nitride and integrated photodiodes, using standard manufacturing flows.
  • The companies said the expanded collaboration is intended to strengthen the manufacturing foundation required for commercial-scale photonic quantum computing, with potential applications spanning quantum computing, data centers, telecommunications, and automotive markets.
  • TSEM -1.24% premarket to $126.75.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Energy Services of America prices $20 million public offering of common stock
data.description:
• Energy Services of America Corporation (ESOA) on Thursday said that it priced an underwritten public offering of 1.74 million shares of common stock at $11.50 per share.
  • The company expects gross proceeds of about $20.0 million before underwriting discounts, commissions and other expenses.
  • It granted the underwriter a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 261,000 shares, which would increase gross proceeds to about $23.0 million if fully exercised.
  • The offering is expected to close on February 20, 2026, subject to customary conditions.
  • Net proceeds are to be used for general corporate purposes, working capital, and potential acquisitions, adding it has no current plans or agreements for any specific acquisition.
  • ESOA -10.29% premarket to $12.9.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Integer Holdings Non-GAAP EPS of $1.76 beats by $0.06, revenue of $472.06M beats by $9.34M
data.description:
• Integer Holdings press release (ITGR): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.76 beats by $0.06.
  • Revenue of $472.06M (+5.0% Y/Y) beats by $9.34M.
  • Cardio & Vascular sales increased 11% to $284 million in the fourth quarter 2025 compared to fourth quarter 2024, driven by the Precision Coating and VSi Parylene acquisitions, and strong demand in Neurovascular. Full year sales increased 17% year-over-year to $1.107 billion, driven by strong growth from new product ramps in Electrophysiology, contributions from acquisitions, and strong demand in Neurovascular.
  • FY26 sales consensus of $1.84B, EPS consensus of $6.30

  • 2026 Outlook(a)

    (dollars in millions, except per share amounts)   GAAP   Non-GAAP(b)
        As Reported   Change fromPrior Year   Adjusted   Change fromPrior Year
    Sales   $1,826 to $1,876   (1)% to 1%   N/A   N/A
    Operating income   $184 to $204   (17)% to (8)%   $304 to $324   (5)% to 1%
